description: Triggered when the user switches focused app. Classifies the focused context (terminal, code editor, chat, browser, email, doc) and emits a hint about what kind of help would be useful here. Read-only.

inputs.name: focused_app
inputs.description: Name of the application that now has focus (e.g. 'Code', 'Terminal', 'Chrome', 'Slack').

inputs.name: previous_app
inputs.description: Name of the app that was focused before (may be empty for the first focus event after boot).

inputs.name: focused_element
inputs.description: Optional shape: {role, name, state[]}. The accessibility element that has keyboard focus inside the app.

outputs.name: contextKind
outputs.description: terminal | editor | browser | chat | email | docs | media | unknown

outputs.name: appFamily
outputs.description: Normalized app family — vscode, jetbrains, chrome, slack, etc.

outputs.name: helpfulnessHint
outputs.description: One-line hint about what kind of proactive help is likely useful in this context.

outputs.name: shouldDeferAction
outputs.description: True iff Parix should hold non-urgent actions while the user is in a deep-focus context (writing in an editor, on a call).
